title: Method for nail manicuring
abstract: A nail manicure method comprising applying a protective coating to portions of the surface of a finger or toe nail or a cuticle adjacent thereto where the application of nail polish is not desired, optionally manipulating the protective coating into a desired shape, and applying nail polish to regions not covered by the protective coating. Once the nail polish has at least partially dried, the protective coating is removed to reveal an underlying area with defined regions of nail polish.
claims: 1. A method of creating a design on a nail surface, the method comprising: i) applying a flowable protective coating over a cuticle surface adjacent the nail surface, the protective coating including natural rubber, synthetic rubber or a combination thereof; ii) allowing the protective coating to at least partially set; iii) applying, by using one or more of a brush, a sponge-tip, or a squeeze dispenser, a first nail polish over the nail surface; iv) applying the flowable protective coating over a first portion of the nail surface on which the first nail polish has been applied; v) allowing the protective coating over the first portion of the nail surface to at least partially set; vi) manipulating the at least partially set protective coating on the first portion of the nail surface on which the first nail polish has been applied into a desired design; vii) applying a second nail polish over a second portion of the nail surface and over at least a portion of the protective coating positioned on the first portion of the nail surface; and viii) removing the at least partially set protective coating from the first portion of the nail surface, wherein the removing the protective coating from the nail surface includes peeling the protective coating from the nail surface; wherein the design formed on the nail surface is defined by distinct colored regions formed in steps iii) and vii).
